description: Render a markdown literature review into a professionally formatted PDF with pandoc (xelatex engine): 1-inch margins, 11pt, colored links, optional table of contents and section numbering, and citeproc bibliography when a sibling .bib exists.
rules: read-only — the source markdown is never modified
if a sibling <review>.bib exists, --citeproc + --csl <style>.csl are added
TOC and section numbering are on by default; set TOC=0 / NUMBER_SECTIONS=0 to disable
the PDF is written to record_store as review.pdf; a run.log captures the pandoc invocation
usage: Set REVIEW_MD (path to the review markdown). Optional: CITATION_STYLE (apa|nature|chicago|vancouver|ieee, default apa), TOC (1/0, default 1), NUMBER_SECTIONS (1/0, default 1). Output: review.pdf (best-effort) + run.log.
limitation: Requires pandoc and xelatex on PATH. When either is absent the porter degrades: run.log records the failure and no PDF is produced (run.log is the guaranteed output the contract checks).
